data.name: PID Temperature Control
data.description: A PID (Proportional-Integral-Derivative) controller precisely regulates the boiler temperature, maintaining accuracy within 0.5 degrees Celsius. This eliminates the temperature surfing required with older thermostat-based machines and ensures shot-to-shot consistency. PID control is considered essential for serious espresso brewing.
benefits: Consistent extraction temperature shot after shot
Adjustable brew temperature for different roast profiles
Eliminates need for temperature surfing
Better flavor clarity and repeatability

data.name: Pressure Profiling
data.description: Pressure profiling allows you to vary the extraction pressure throughout the shot, rather than maintaining a flat 9 bars. By controlling the pressure curve, you can emphasize different flavor characteristics: a gentle ramp-up reduces channeling, while a declining profile can enhance sweetness and body. This is the frontier of espresso extraction.
benefits: Extract a wider range of flavors from each coffee
Reduce channeling with gentle pre-infusion pressure
Enhance sweetness with declining pressure profiles
Replicate famous extraction profiles from competition baristas
Save and recall custom profiles

data.name: Dual Boiler System
data.description: A dual boiler system uses separate boilers for brewing and steaming, each with independent temperature control. This means you can brew espresso and steam milk simultaneously without any temperature compromise. The brew boiler stays at the optimal extraction temperature while the steam boiler runs hotter for powerful, dry steam.
benefits: Brew and steam simultaneously
No recovery time between shots and steaming
Independent temperature control for each function
Faster workflow, especially for milk drinks
More consistent results under heavy use

data.name: Flat Burr Grinder
data.description: Flat burrs consist of two parallel disc-shaped burrs that slice coffee beans into highly uniform particles. The result is a cleaner, brighter cup with more clarity and definition in the flavor. Flat burrs are favored by those who want to taste the distinct origin character of their coffee with precision and transparency.
benefits: Superior particle size uniformity
Cleaner, brighter cup with more clarity
Better definition of origin flavors
Preferred for light and medium roast espresso
More even extraction across the puck

data.name: Conical Burr Grinder
data.description: Conical burrs use a cone-shaped inner burr that fits inside a ring-shaped outer burr. The geometry produces a bimodal particle distribution that many people find creates a sweeter, fuller-bodied cup with more perceived richness. Conical burrs tend to run cooler and quieter than flat burrs.
benefits: Sweeter, fuller-bodied cup profile
Quieter operation at lower RPM
Less heat generated during grinding
Excellent all-rounder for espresso and filter
Naturally lower retention

data.name: Zero Retention Design
data.description: Zero retention grinders are designed so that virtually every milligram of coffee you put in comes back out as ground coffee. Traditional hopper grinders retain 2-5g of stale grounds between uses. A zero retention design, typically using a bellows system and vertical burr alignment, ensures every dose is 100% fresh.
benefits: Every dose is ground fresh from whole beans
No stale coffee mixing into fresh doses
Accurate dosing by weight (what goes in comes out)
Easy to switch between different beans
Less waste over time

data.name: Saturated Group Head
data.description: A saturated group head is directly connected to and heated by the brew boiler, meaning the group is always at the exact same temperature as the water. Unlike E61 group heads that rely on thermosyphon circulation, a saturated group provides the most stable and consistent temperature delivery to the coffee puck, shot after shot.
benefits: Most thermally stable group head design
Exact temperature match between boiler and group
Excellent shot-to-shot consistency
Faster recovery between back-to-back shots
Preferred by competition baristas

data.name: Flow Control
data.description: Flow control allows you to manually adjust the flow rate of water through the coffee puck during extraction. Using a paddle or needle valve, you can perform a slow pre-infusion, ramp up to full flow, and taper off at the end. This provides a different dimension of control compared to pressure profiling and can produce uniquely sweet, complex shots.
benefits: Manual control over water flow rate
Extended low-pressure pre-infusion
Ability to create unique extraction profiles
Reduce channeling with slow initial flow
Explore a wider range of flavors from each coffee

data.name: Automatic Milk System
data.description: An automatic milk system froths and steams milk to the perfect temperature and texture at the touch of a button. Using a dedicated milk container connected to the machine, it dispenses perfectly textured milk directly into your cup. This removes the learning curve of manual steam wand technique while delivering consistent milk drinks every time.
benefits: Perfect milk texture every time with no technique required
One-touch operation for milk drinks
Consistent results regardless of operator skill
Automatic cleaning rinse after each use
Ideal for shared environments like offices

data.name: Touchscreen Interface
data.description: A full-color touchscreen interface replaces traditional buttons and dials with an intuitive digital control surface. Save custom extraction profiles, view real-time pressure and flow graphs, adjust temperatures, and manage maintenance schedules all from the screen. Firmware updates keep your machine at the cutting edge.
benefits: Intuitive, modern user interface
Save and recall custom extraction profiles
Real-time extraction data visualization
Easy access to maintenance and diagnostics
Firmware updates for new features over time

data.name: Plumbed-In Water Connection
data.description: A plumbed-in water connection provides a continuous water supply directly from your home or office plumbing. This eliminates the need to refill a water tank, enables continuous operation for high-volume use, and typically includes integrated water filtration for optimal water quality and machine protection.
benefits: No manual water tank refilling
Continuous operation for high-volume environments
Consistent water pressure for extraction
Integrated water filtration options
Professional installation ensures optimal water quality
